name: Beastmen Brayherds
sources: Warhammer: The Old World — Ravening Hordes (2024)

army_compositions.name: Beastmen Brayherds Grand Army

rules.notes: 0-1 Beastlord, Great Bray-Shaman or Doombull per 1,000 points; Wargors, Bray-Shamans, Gorebulls and Centigor Chieftains as desired
0-1 Beastlord, Great Bray-Shaman or Doombull per 1,000 points (combined limit)

1+ Gor Herd; Ungor Herds, Chaos Warhounds, Razorgor Herds and Tuskgor Chariots; 0-1 Bestigor Herd as Core if General is Beastlord or Wargor; 0-1 Minotaur Herd as Core if General is Doombull or Gorebull
0-1 Bestigor Herd may be taken as a Core choice if your General is a Beastlord or Wargor
0-1 Minotaur Herd may be taken as a Core choice if your General is a Doombull or Gorebull

Bestigor Herds, Harpies, Minotaur Herds, Centigor Herds, Dragon Ogres, Razorgor Chariots and Cockatrice; Chaos Ogres (see Warriors of Chaos army list); 0-1 Jabberslythe or Cygor as Special if General is Great Bray-Shaman or Bray-Shaman; 0-1 Ghorgon as Special if General is Doombull or Gorebull
0-1 Jabberslythe or Cygor may be taken as a Special choice if your General is a Great Bray-Shaman or Bray-Shaman
0-1 Ghorgon may be taken as a Special choice if your General is a Doombull or Gorebull
Dragon Ogre Shaggoths, Jabberslythes, Cygors, Ghorgons and Chaos Giants; Chaos Trolls and Chaos Spawn (see Warriors of Chaos army list)
Any Beastmen Brayherds Army of Infamy composition list; or one of: Orc & Goblin Tribes (Uneasy), Warriors of Chaos, Tomb Kings of Khemri (Suspicious)

army_compositions.battle_standard_bearer: A single Wargor in your army may be upgraded to be your Battle Standard Bearer for +25 points. In addition to their usual allowance of points to spend on magic items, a Battle Standard Bearer can purchase a single magic standard with no points limit.

army_compositions.name: Beastmen Brayherds — Minotaur Blood Herd

rules.notes: 0-1 Doombull per 1,000 points; 0-1 Bray-Shaman per 1,000 points; Gorebulls, Wargors and Centigor Chieftains as desired
0-1 Doombull per 1,000 points
0-1 Bray-Shaman per 1,000 points
1 Minotaur Herd per 1,000 points; 0-1 Herdstone; Gor Herds, Ungor Herds and Chaos Warhounds
1 Minotaur Herd per 1,000 points
0-1 unit of Dragon Ogres per 1,000 points; Harpies, Minotaur Herds, Centigor Herds, Razorgor Herds and Ghorgons
0-1 unit of Dragon Ogres per 1,000 points
0-1 Dragon Ogre Shaggoth per 1,000 points; Chaos Giants; Chaos Ogres and Chaos Trolls (see Ravening Hordes)
0-1 Dragon Ogre Shaggoth per 1,000 points

special_rules.name: Bloodshed & Butchery
special_rules.description: All Doombulls, Gorebulls, Minotaur Herds and Gor Herds within a Minotaur Blood Herd army gain the Impetuous special rule.

special_rules.name: Champions Of Slaughter
special_rules.description: If the General of a Minotaur Blood Herd becomes Frenzied as a result of the Blood Rage special rule, any friendly units within its Command range will also become Frenzied.

special_rules.name: Guardians Of The Herdstones
special_rules.description: Doombulls and Gorebulls in a Minotaur Blood Herd may take Chaos Mutations and/or Gifts of Chaos up to a total of 50 points. This replaces the option to take Chaos Mutations as shown in Ravening Hordes.

special_rules.name: Magics Of The Dark Gods
special_rules.description: The General of a Minotaur Blood Herd must be a Doombull or Gorebull. For +35 points, this character may be a Level 1 Wizard, or for +65 points, this character may be a Level 2 Wizard. If so, this character knows spells from the Lore of Battle Magic or the Lore of Dark Magic. A Wizard with this special rule may wear armour without penalty.

army_compositions.battle_standard_bearer: A single Gorebull or Wargor in your army may be upgraded to be your Battle Standard Bearer for +25 points. In addition to their usual allowance of points to spend on magic items, a Battle Standard Bearer can purchase a single magic standard with no points limit.

army_compositions.name: Beastmen Brayherds — Wild Herd

rules.notes: 0-1 Beastlord or Great Bray-Shaman per 1,000 points; Wargors, Bray-Shamans and Centigor Chieftains as desired
0-1 Beastlord or Great Bray-Shaman per 1,000 points (combined limit)
1+ Primal Warherd; 0-1 Tuskgor Chariot per 1,000 points; 0-1 Centigor Herd as Core per Centigor Chieftain taken; Gor Herds, Ungor Herds, Chaos Warhounds, Primal Warhounds and Razorgor Herds
1+ Primal Warherd
0-1 Tuskgor Chariot per 1,000 points
0-1 unit of Dragon Ogres; Warped Gors, Bestigor Herds, Harpies, Centigor Herds, Tuskgor Chariots and Razorgor Chariots; 0-1 Cockatrice or Preyton per 1,000 points
0-1 Cockatrice or Preyton per 1,000 points (combined limit)
Dragon Ogre Shaggoths, Jabberslythes, Cygors, Chaos Giants and Chaos Spawn

special_rules.name: Ancient Sorcery
special_rules.description: In addition to the Lores of Magic they may normally know spells from, Beastmen Shamans within a Wild Herd army may know spells from the Lore of Primal Magic.

special_rules.name: Dark Coven
special_rules.description: Beastmen Shamans within a Wild Herd army may leach power from nearby Wizards when they attempt to cast a spell or when attempting a Wizardly Dispel. A Bray-Shaman or Great Bray-Shaman may apply a +1 modifier to their Casting or Dispel roll for each other friendly Wizard within their Command range. However, if they roll any natural double when making the roll, something has gone horribly wrong and the spell is miscast or they have been outclassed in the art. Roll immediately on the Miscast table and apply the result to the Bray-Shaman or Great Bray-Shaman that made the attempt and to each friendly Wizard within their Command range.

special_rules.name: Gifts From The Dark Gods
special_rules.description: Character models in this army may spend an additional 25 points on magic items and an additional 25 points on Chaos Mutations.

special_rules.name: Roaming Warbands
special_rules.description: Within a Wild Herd army, any number of Gor Herds, Ungor Herds, Primal Warherds and Centigor Herds may have the Ambushers special rule at the cost of +1 point per model. Any number of Beastmen Chieftains and Centigor Chieftains may have the Ambushers special rule at the cost of +10 points per model. In addition, 0-1 Gor Herd, Ungor Herd or Centigor Herd per 1,000 points with a Unit Strength of 10 or less may be given the Ambushers special rule for free.

army_compositions.battle_standard_bearer: A single Wargor in your army may be upgraded to be your Battle Standard Bearer for +25 points. In addition to their usual allowance of points to spend on magic items, a Battle Standard Bearer can purchase a single magic standard with no points limit.

notes: The Quickening Storm: This model has a 5+ Ward save against wounds from Magic Missiles or Magical Vortex spells. If this model, or the unit it belongs to, suffers one or more hits from Storm Call, it becomes 'Quickened' (+1 Initiative and Attacks until next Start of Turn sub-phase).

units.name: Chaos Warhounds
equipment: Claws and fangs (counts as hand weapon)

units.name: Tuskgor Chariot
notes: Character Mount: A Tuskgor Chariot may be included in your army as a character's mount. If so, its points are added to that of its rider.

units.name: Razorgor Chariot
notes: Character Mount: A Razorgor Chariot may be included in your army as a character's mount. If so, its points are added to that of its rider.

units.name: Dragon Ogre Shaggoth

options.condition: May take one of the following
May take one of the following

options.description: Replace light armour with heavy armour

options.notes: May take Chaos Mutations up to a total of 50 points
May purchase magic items up to a total of 50 points

notes: Storm Call: Bound spell, Power Level 1. Type: Magic Missile. Casting Value: 7+. Range: Self. Effect: All units within 6" of this model (friend or foe), including units engaged in combat and this model, suffer D3 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -1.

units.name: Jabberslythe
notes: Wicked claws: Range Combat, S=S, AP=-2, no special rules.
Slythey tongue: Range 12", S=5, AP=-1, Special Rules: Move & Shoot, Quick Shot.

Maddening Aura: During the Command sub-phase of its turn, any enemy unit within 8" of this model (including fleeing or engaged units) must make a Leadership test. If failed, the unit suffers D3 Strength 3 hits, no armour or Regeneration saves permitted (Ward saves allowed).
Spurting Bile Blood: For each Wound this model loses during the Combat phase, the attacking enemy unit suffers a Strength 4 hit, with an AP of -1.

units.name: Cockatrice

options.notes: Acidic vomit: Range N/A, S=3, AP=-1, Breath Weapon

notes: Petrifying gaze: Range 18", S=2, AP=N/A, Special Rules: Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (D3). When making a roll To Wound for an attack made with this weapon, substitute the target's Toughness with its Initiative. No armour save is permitted against wounds caused by this weapon (Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).

Stony Stare: At the start of each Combat phase, enemy models in base contact with this model must make an Initiative test. If this test is failed, they suffer D3 Strength 2 hits, with no armour save permitted (Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).

units.name: Cygor
notes: Hurl attack: Range 12-36", S=4(8), AP=-1(-3), Special Rules: Bombardment, Cumbersome, Multiple Wounds (D3+1). This weapon shoots like a stone thrower, using the 'Bombardment' special rule and a 3" blast template. If a 'Misfire' is rolled on the Artillery dice, this model loses a single Wound (instead of rolling on a Misfire table). The Multiple Wounds (D3+1) special rule applies only to a single model whose base lies underneath the central hole of the blast template.

Ghostsight: During the Combat phase, a Cygor may re-roll any failed Rolls To Hit made against enemy Wizards, enemy models or units equipped with any magic items, or enemy models or units with a Ward or Regeneration save.

Soul-eater: Any enemy Wizard that wishes to cast a spell whilst within 12" of one or more Cygors must first make a Leadership test. If failed, the Wizard has lost their nerve and, should they fail to cast the spell, the spell has been miscast.

units.name: Chaos Giant
notes: Giant's club: Stats and special rules vary depending on what the Giant does with it, as described in the Giant Attacks special rule.

units.name: Ghorgon
notes: Cleaver-limbs: Range Combat, S=S, AP=-2, Special Rules: Killing Blow, Monster Slayer.

Swallow Whole: During the Command sub-phase of its turn, a Ghorgon that is engaged in combat with one or more units of 'regular infantry' or 'heavy infantry' may choose to make a 'Swallow Whole' attack against a nominated unit. The unit must make an Initiative test. If failed, a single model is removed as a casualty. Each time an enemy model is removed from play in this way, the Ghorgon recovers a single lost Wound.

units.name: Herdstone
notes: 0-1 per army. May be taken as a Special choice in any Beastmen Brayherds army made using any army composition list.
An impassable terrain feature placed anywhere on the battlefield not within the opponent's deployment zone and not within 6" of the centre of the battlefield.
Controlled by a single unit within 6" that is not fleeing and has a Unit Strength of five or more.
A Tingle in the Air: The unit that controls the Herdstone gains Magic Resistance (-1).
Bestial Fury: If controlled by a Beastmen Brayherds unit, friendly Beastmen Brayherds units within 12" may re-roll rolls To Wound of a natural 1 made whilst engaged in combat.

Dark Sorcery: If controlled by a Beastmen Brayherds unit, friendly Beastmen Brayherds Wizards within 12" gain a +1 modifier to their Casting rolls, and enemy Wizards within 12" suffer a -1 modifier to their Casting rolls.

Fearsome Edifice: Whilst within 12" of a Herdstone controlled by a Beastmen Brayherds unit, enemy units suffer a -1 modifier to their Leadership when making a Leadership test due to Fear, Panic or Terror.
Victory Points: Controlling player is awarded a bonus of 200 Victory Points.

units.name: Warped Gors

options.condition: The entire unit must take one of the following (free)
The entire unit must take one of the following (free)
0-1 unit may

notes: Blessings Of Chaos: When this unit's combat is chosen during Step 1.1 of any Choose & Fight Combat sub-phase, roll on the Blessings Of Chaos Table: 1-2 Stinging Barbs (Poisoned Attacks until end of Combat phase); 3-4 Scything Talons (AP -2 on all attacks until end of Combat phase); 5-6 Chitinous Hide (+1 Toughness until end of Combat phase).
Wild Herd composition only.

units.name: Primal Warherd

options.condition: Gors must take one of the following (free)
Gors must take one of the following (free)
Ungors must take one of the following (free)
Ungors must take one of the following (free)

options.description: Replace a hand weapon or shield with a great weapon for Foe-render
options.notes: A Foe-render may replace a hand weapon or its shield with a great weapon for free
A Foe-render may purchase magic items up to a total of 25 points

options.description: Purchase a magic standard worth up to 50 points

notes: Must contain at least ten Gors and ten Ungors. Gors always occupy the front ranks, with Ungors making up the rear ranks.

Mixed Unit: Before rolling To Wound, hits caused by enemy shooting should be divided as evenly as possible between Gors and Ungors. In combat, enemy models must direct their attacks against models in the fighting rank of this unit.
Wild Herd composition only.

units.name: Primal Warhounds
equipment: Claws and fangs (counts as hand weapons)

options.description: Include one Ungor Ravager for every three Primal Warhounds
options.notes: 7 points each per Ungor Ravager added

notes: Wild Herd composition only.

Ungor Ravager: WS 3, BS 3, S 3, T 3, W 1, I 3, A 1, Ld 7. Regular infantry. 25 x 25 mm base. Equipment: Hand weapons, throwing spears and shields. Special Rules: Close Order, Horde, Leader of the Pack, Move through Cover, Primal Fury, Swiftstride, Warband.
Leader of the Pack: Models with this rule are positioned at the rear rank(s) of the Primal Warhounds unit. Primal Warhounds always occupy the front rank(s).

units.name: Preyton
equipment: Claws and fangs (counts as hand weapon)
notes: Twisted antlers: Range Combat, S=S, AP=-2, Special Rules: Armour Bane (1).
Crown of Antlers: Impact Hits caused by a model with this special rule have the Armour Bane (1) special rule and an Armour Piercing characteristic of -1.

Endless Malice: If this model wins a round of combat and chooses to restrain and reform (and passes its Leadership test to do so), enemy units within 6" of it must make a Panic test as if a nearby friend had been destroyed.
Grand Army: 0-1 Preyton per 1,000 points as a Special choice. Wild Herd: 0-1 Cockatrice or Preyton per 1,000 points as a Special choice.

units.name: Kralmaw, the Prophet of Ruin
units.base_size: 30 x 30 mm (Kralmaw); 25 x 25 mm (Leering Spirit marker)
notes: May be included in any Beastmen Brayherds army made using any army composition list that includes this option. Must be fielded as presented here.
Future Sight: Kralmaw has a 4+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.

Leering Spirit: When Kralmaw is deployed, place a Leering Spirit marker anywhere on the battlefield not within 8" of an enemy unit. The marker cannot be charged, targeted or attacked in any way. If Kralmaw is removed from play as a casualty, the marker is also removed. During the Shooting phase of each of Kralmaw's turns, the Leering Spirit marker may cast the Devolve spell from the Lore of Beasts as a Bound Spell with a Power Level of 3.
Grisly Totem: Counts as a Braystaff. In addition, all enemy units within 6" of Kralmaw suffer a -1 modifier to their Leadership characteristic (to a minimum of 2).

units.name: Ghorros Warhoof, Sire of a Thousand Young
equipment: The Skull of the Unicorn Lord (see notes)
notes: May be included in any Beastmen Brayherds army made using any army composition list that includes this option. Must be fielded as presented here.
Mansmasher: Range Combat, S=S+1, AP=-1, Special Rules: Armour Bane (1), Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (D3). This weapon's Strength modifier applies only during a turn in which its wielder charged.

The Skull of the Unicorn Lord: Ghorros has a 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a non-magical enemy attack and a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a magical enemy attack.
Father Of Beasts: Whilst within Ghorros Warhoof's Command range, friendly Centigor Herds may re-roll the D6 when rolling on the Drunken table.

The Sons of Ghorros: 0-1 Centigor herd in the same muster list as Ghorros may be upgraded to Sons of Ghorros for +2 points per model. Sons of Ghorros have a +1 modifier to their Weapon Skill and Leadership characteristics (to a maximum of 10) and gain the Armour Bane (1) special rule.

chaos_mutations.name: Slug-skin
chaos_mutations.description: Any enemy model that directs its attacks against this model or any unit it has joined during the Combat phase suffers a -1 modifier to its rolls To Hit.

chaos_mutations.name: Crown of Horns
chaos_mutations.description: This character and any unit they have joined gains the Stubborn special rule.

chaos_mutations.name: Muscular Monstrosity
chaos_mutations.description: This model (but not its mount) has a +1 modifier to its Strength characteristic.

chaos_mutations.name: Pelt of Midnight
chaos_mutations.description: Any enemy model that targets this character or any unit they have joined during the Shooting phase suffers an additional -1 To Hit modifier.

chaos_mutations.name: Gouge-tusks
chaos_mutations.description: During the Combat phase, the Armour Piercing characteristic of any weapon used by this model (but not its mount) is improved by 1.

chaos_mutations.name: Rune of the Beast Ascendant
chaos_mutations.description: Unless this character is fleeing, friendly Bestigor, Gor and Ungor Herds gain a +1 modifier to their Leadership characteristic whilst within this character's Command range (to a maximum of 10).

chaos_mutations.name: Many-limbed Fiend
chaos_mutations.description: This model (but not its mount) has a +1 modifier to its Attacks characteristic. However, this bonus attack must be made using an ordinary hand weapon.

chaos_mutations.name: Gnarled Hide
chaos_mutations.description: This model gains the Armoured Hide (1) special rule.

chaos_mutations.name: Uncanny Senses
chaos_mutations.description: This model (but not its mount) has a +1 modifier to its Initiative characteristic.

magic_items.name: Dragonblade
magic_items.description: Dragonblade is inscribed with a Rune of Fury and a Grudge Rune, as described in Forces of Fantasy.

magic_items.name: Burning Blade*
magic_items.description:
Once unsheathed, the cutting edge of the Burning Blade writhes endlessly with a living flame.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-Flaming AttacksMagical Attacks*Extremely Common
*Extremely Common

magic_items.description:
The serrated length of the Biting Blade cuts through armour of plate and mail with terrible ease.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-2Armour Bane (1)Magical Attacks

magic_items.description:
Engraved with runes of accuracy and swiftness, the Sword of Striking is an elegant weapon of great precision.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-Magical AttacksNotes: During the Combat phase, the wielder of the Sword of Striking has a +1 modifier to their rolls To Hit.*Extremely Common

magic_items.description:
Quenched in the blood of untold fallen enemies, the Berserker Blade eternally hungers for battle.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-Extra Attacks (+1)ImpetuousMagical AttacksNotes: The wielder of the Berserker Blade is Impetuous.
Notes: The wielder of the Berserker Blade is Impetuous.

magic_items.description:
Bound around with powerful runes that increase its power and enhance the strength of its bearer.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Magical Attacks*Extremely Common
*Extremely Common

magic_items.description:
Crafted from magically attuned materials and ensorcelled to strike as if with a life of its own.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-Magical AttacksStrike First

magic_items.name: Giant Blade
magic_items.description:
A broad and heavy blade wrought with powerful enchantments that only increase the weight of its blows, the Giant Blade is an unsubtle weapon.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-Armour Bane (2)Magical AttacksMultiple Wounds (2)

magic_items.name: Hell-forged Axe
magic_items.description:
In the midst of battle, smoke billows and flames burst from the glowing blade of this fell weapon.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Armour Bane (3)Flaming AttacksMagical Attacks

magic_items.name: Sword of Sorrow
magic_items.description: Though it possesses no special properties when wielded in combat, it is said that a gesture with the Sword of Sorrow can pierce an enemy's heart from afar.
magic_items.restrictions: Sword of Sorrow(Magic Weapon)30 pointsThough it possesses no special properties when wielded in combat, it is said that a gesture with the Sword of Sorrow can pierce an enemy's heart from afar.RangeSt

magic_items.name: Spelleater Axe
magic_items.description:
Forged of rune-encrusted brass, the Spelleater Axe is anathema to the Winds of Magic.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-1Magical AttacksMagic Resistance (-2)

magic_items.name: Headsman's Axe
magic_items.description:
A wide bladed axe, steeped in the blood of its countless helpless victims.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Killing BlowMagical AttacksRequires Two Hands

magic_items.name: Dragon Slaying Sword
magic_items.description:
A mighty blade, wielded by many great heroes in their endless quests to slay foul monsters.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-Magical AttacksMonster Slayer

magic_items.name: Meteor Hammer
magic_items.description:
No matter who wields this mighty hammer, their blows will land with the force of a meteor plummeting from the heavens.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-3Magical AttacksRequires Two HandsNotes: When making a roll To Wound with this weapon, a roll of a 3+ is always a success, regardless of the target's Toughness.

magic_items.name: Duellist's Blades
magic_items.description:
Fine weapons, forged by the greatest of swordsmiths and imbued with potent runes of quickening.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS-1Extra Attacks (+2)Magical AttacksRequires Two Hands

magic_items.name: Sword of Battle
magic_items.description:
A simple weapon, yet finely crafted, blessed by mighty mages and revered priests.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Armour Bane (1)Extra Attacks (+1)Magical Attacks

magic_items.name: Cackling Blade
magic_items.description:
Those who carry the Cackling Blade relinquish all self control until the battle has long ended - often to their peril.
RangeStrengthArmour PiercingSpecial RulesCombatS+1-1Extra Attacks (+D6)Magical AttacksNotes: If a natural 6 is rolled for the Extra Attacks (+D6) special rule, the wielder of the Cackling Blade immediately loses a single Wound.

magic_items.name: Ogre Blade
magic_items.description:
Magic weapons grant the wielder extra abilities, new special rules or an improved profile, the better to slay enemies. Unless otherwise stated, the following rules apply to all magic weapons:
A magic weapon cannot be used alongside an additional hand weapon, magical or mundane, to gain an extra attack.
A character that wields a magic weapon cannot set it aside to use a mundane equivalent. For example, a character cannot set aside a magic combat weapon to use a mundane great weapon instead.
magic_items.restrictions: A magic weapon cannot be used alongside an additional hand weapon, magical or mundane, to gain an extra attack.A character that wields a magic weapon cannot set it aside to use a mundane equivalent. F

magic_items.name: Charmed Shield*
magic_items.description: A simple shield hung with charms and fetishes to bring the bearer good fortune. In the heat of battle, such ornamentation rarely survives intact for long.

magic_items.name: Enchanted Shield*
magic_items.description:
A finely crafted and sturdy shield that protects its bearer from harm time and time again.
The Enchanted Shield is a shield. In addition, its bearer has a 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a non-magical enemy attack.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Armour of Meteoric Iron
magic_items.description:
This heavy armour was forged of ore mined from a meteor that fell from the heavens.
The Armour of Meteoric Iron gives its wearer an armour value of 5+, which cannot be improved in any way. However, nor can this armour value be reduced in any way either.
magic_items.restrictions: Armour of Meteoric Iron(Magic Armour)20 pointsThis heavy armour was forged of ore mined from a meteor that fell from the heavens.The Armour of Meteoric Iron gives its wearer an armour value of 5+, whi

magic_items.name: Trailblazer's Hauberk
magic_items.description:
The local flora seems to shift and bend to allow the wearer to pass unimpeded through even the thickest briar.
Models whose troop type is infantry only. The Trailblazer's Hauberk is a suit of heavy armour. In addition, its wearer gains the Move Through Cover and Scouts special rules.
magic_items.restrictions: Trailblazer's Hauberk(Magic Armour)20 pointsThe local flora seems to shift and bend to allow the wearer to pass unimpeded through even the thickest briar.Models whose troop type is infantry only. The

magic_items.name: Levitating Shield
magic_items.description:
Born aloft by a cunning enchantment, this shield seems to place itself between its owner and the weapons of the enemy.
Models whose troop type is infantry only. The Levitating Shield is a shield. In addition, the bearer may use it alongside a weapon with the Requires Two Hands special rule during combat.
magic_items.restrictions: Levitating Shield(Magic Armour)25 pointsBorn aloft by a cunning enchantment, this shield seems to place itself between its owner and the weapons of the enemy.Models whose troop type is infantry only.

magic_items.name: Spellshield*
magic_items.description:
The Winds of Magic lose their power when in the presence of the brass-bound Spellshield.
The Spellshield is a shield. In addition, its bearer has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a Magic Missile, a Magical Vortex, or an Assailment spell.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Shield of the Warrior True
magic_items.description:
A heavy shield of oak and iron that turns aside all but the most deadly of missiles.
The Shield of the Warrior True is a shield. In addition, its bearer has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered during the Shooting phase.

magic_items.name: Glittering Scales
magic_items.description:
Each and every scale of this armoured surcoat is crafted from shards of precious metal.
The Glittering Scales is a suit of light armour. In addition, once per turn, you may make your opponent re-roll a single roll To Hit made against the wearer.

magic_items.name: Armour of Silvered Steel
magic_items.description:
Polished to a mirror shine, the Armour of Silvered Steel encases its wearer from head to toe.
The Armour of Silvered Steel is a suit of armour that gives its wearer an armour value of 3+ which cannot be improved in any way.
magic_items.restrictions: Armour of Silvered Steel(Magic Armour)40 pointsPolished to a mirror shine, the Armour of Silvered Steel encases its wearer from head to toe.The Armour of Silvered Steel is a suit of armour that gives

magic_items.name: Bedazzling Helm
magic_items.description: A gleaming helm that confuses and confounds enemies, causing their blows to fall wide of their mark.
magic_items.restrictions: Bedazzling Helm(Magic Armour)60 pointsA gleaming helm that confuses and confounds enemies, causing their blows to fall wide of their mark.Models whose troop type is infantry or cavalry only. May be wo

magic_items.name: Armour of Destiny
magic_items.description: Only a model that is equipped with (or that has the option to be equipped with) armour, a shield or barding may purchase a magical equivalent. A model can only wear a single suit of armour and can only carry one shield, and a mount can only wear a single suit of barding. Therefore, magic armour replaces any mundane equivalent the model is, or may be, equipped with.
magic_items.restrictions: Only a model that is equipped with (or that has the option to be equipped with) armour, a shield or barding may purchase a magical equivalent. A model can only wear a single suit of armour and can onl

magic_items.name: Luckstone*
magic_items.description:
Can luck be trapped within a dull stone pendant? Possibly.
Single use. The bearer of a Luckstone can re-roll a single failed Armour Save roll.*Extremely Common
Single use. The bearer of a Luckstone can re-roll a single failed Armour Save roll.
*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Obsidian Lodestone*
magic_items.description:
An air of chill hangs about obsidian, stilling the Winds of Magic in its presence.
A model may purchase up to three Obsidian Lodestones. A model that bears a single Obsidian Lodestone has Magic Resistance (-1), a model that bears two has Magic Resistance (-2), and a model that bears three has Magic Resistance (-3).*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Paymaster's Coin*
magic_items.description:
This heavy golden coin may not be enchanted, but its weight in its owner's pocket certainly inspires heroism.
Single use. The bearer of a Paymaster's Coin can re-roll any failed rolls To Hit made during the Combat phase.*Extremely Common
Single use. The bearer of a Paymaster's Coin can re-roll any failed rolls To Hit made during the Combat phase.
*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Ironhide Talisman
magic_items.description:
It is said that the one who bears this powerful heirloom can endure even the most telling blows.
Models whose troop type is infantry only. Any enemy model that makes a successful roll To Hit against the bearer of the Ironhide Talisman during the Shooting phase or Combat phase must re-roll any rolls To Wound of a natural 6.Note that this magic item does not work against attacks that hit automatically.
magic_items.restrictions: Ironhide Talisman(Talisman)30 pointsIt is said that the one who bears this powerful heirloom can endure even the most telling blows.Models whose troop type is infantry only. Any enemy model that makes

magic_items.name: Talisman of Protection
magic_items.description:
A powerful magical aura surrounds this humble talisman, protecting its wearer from harm.
The Talisman of Protection gives its bearer a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.

magic_items.name: Dawnstone
magic_items.description:
Talismans are tokens of magical protection, worn by warriors to protect them from harm both physical and magical.
The magic trapped within this semi-precious stone binds together shattered armour plates.
The bearer of the Dawnstone may re-roll any Armour Save roll of a natural 1.
magic_items.restrictions: Dawnstone(Talisman)35 pointsThe magic trapped within this semi-precious stone binds together shattered armour plates.The bearer of the Dawnstone may re-roll any Armour Save roll of a natural 1.Icon of

magic_items.name: Icon of Fortitude
magic_items.description:
Carefully woven with protective enchantments, this exquisite brooch is said to shield its bearer from the deadliest blows.
Models whose troop type is infantry or cavalry only. The bearer is immune to the Multiple Wounds (X) special rule. If the bearer is wounded by an attack with this special rule, they suffer a single wound.
magic_items.restrictions: Icon of Fortitude(Talisman)35 pointsCarefully woven with protective enchantments, this exquisite brooch is said to shield its bearer from the deadliest blows.Models whose troop type is infantry or cav

magic_items.name: Potion of Foolhardiness*
magic_items.description:
A magical brew that fills the drinker with courage… it may actually just be strong liquor.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Potion of Foolhardiness can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model gains the Immune To Psychology special rule.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Potion of Speed*
magic_items.description:
A powerful potion with a terrible aftertaste that sharpens the wits and speeds the reactions.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Potion of Speed can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model has a +D3 modifier to its Initiative characteristic (to a maximum of 10).*Extremely Common
magic_items.restrictions: Potion of Speed*(Enchanted Item)10 pointsA powerful potion with a terrible aftertaste that sharpens the wits and speeds the reactions.Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer

magic_items.name: Becalming Orb
magic_items.description:
This pulsing crystal orb can syphon the Winds of Magic away from its bearer's enemies.
Single Use. The bearer of the Becalming Orb may cast the following Bound spell, with a Power level of 1:TypeHexCasting Value8+RangeSelfUntil your next Start of Turn sub-phase, enemy Wizards that are within 15" of the caster when attempting to cast a spell must increase that spell's casting value by 2.

magic_items.name: Potion of Toughness*
magic_items.description:
Distilled by magical means, this potion is sure to make the drinker all but invulnerable.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Potion of Toughness can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model has a +D3 modifier to its Toughness characteristic (to a maximum of 10).*Extremely Common
magic_items.restrictions: Potion of Toughness*(Enchanted Item)20 pointsDistilled by magical means, this potion is sure to make the drinker all but invulnerable.Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer

magic_items.name: Potion of Strength*
magic_items.description:
A concoction of strength-giving vegetables and roots, mixed together with a touch of magic.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Potion of Strength can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model has a +D3 modifier to its Strength characteristic (to a maximum of 10).*Extremely Common
magic_items.restrictions: Potion of Strength*(Enchanted Item)25 pointsA concoction of strength-giving vegetables and roots, mixed together with a touch of magic.Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the beare

magic_items.name: Potion of Fervour
magic_items.description:
This noxious brew allows those who risk drinking it a moment of godliness upon the battlefield.
Single Use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of the Potion of Fervour can consume it. Until the end of that turn, the model has a +D3 modifier to its Attacks characteristic (to a maximum of 10).Note that a model can only consume a single potion (of any type) per Command phase.
magic_items.restrictions: Potion of Fervour(Enchanted Item)30 pointsThis noxious brew allows those who risk drinking it a moment of godliness upon the battlefield.Single Use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bea

magic_items.name: Healing Potion*
magic_items.description:
Purveyors of magical potions make many bold claims. Sometimes they are true, sometimes not.
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Healing Potion can consume it. The model immediately recovers D3 lost Wounds.*Extremely Common
Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Healing Potion can consume it. The model immediately recovers D3 lost Wounds.

magic_items.name: Ruby Ring of Ruin
magic_items.description:
Uttering a simple command, the wearer of the ring can hurl a flaming ball of ruin at their foes.
The wielder of the Ruby Ring of Ruin can cast the Fireball spell from the Lore of Battle Magic as a Bound spell, with a Power Level of 1.

magic_items.name: Arch-Lightning Rod
magic_items.description: Crackling lightning fills the heavens with peril, grounding the creatures of the sky.
magic_items.restrictions: Arch-Lightning Rod(Enchanted Item)40 pointsCrackling lightning fills the heavens with peril, grounding the creatures of the sky.Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, if they are not

magic_items.name: Flying Carpet
magic_items.description:
Woven from the finest silks in a far-off land, this splendid rug does more than warm the feet!
Models whose troop type is regular infantry or heavy infantry only. The bearer of the Flying Carpet gains the Fly (8) and Swiftstride special rules. However, the bearer cannot join a unit.
magic_items.restrictions: Flying Carpet(Enchanted Item)40 pointsWoven from the finest silks in a far-off land, this splendid rug does more than warm the feet!Models whose troop type is regular infantry or heavy infantry only.

magic_items.name: Wizarding Hat
magic_items.description:
The enchanted items category includes all manner of wonderful artefacts too unique or specific to be included in another category. Enchanted items are often amongst the most prized magical items a character might possess.
Note that a model can only consume a single potion (of any type) per Command phase.
This splendid hat, once the property of a long dead Wizard, is still haunted by their ghost.
magic_items.restrictions: The enchanted items category includes all manner of wonderful artefacts too unique or specific to be included in another category. Enchanted items are often amongst the most prized magical items a cha

magic_items.name: Earthing Rod
magic_items.description:
Inert metal runs the length of the Wizard's staff, drawing magical overloads safely into the earth.
Single use. Should they miscast a spell, a Wizard can use the Earthing Rod to re-roll the result rolled on the Miscast table.

magic_items.name: Arcane Familiar
magic_items.description: Many Wizards are accompanied by familiars gifted with arcane knowledge. These creatures constantly whisper secrets to their master.

magic_items.name: Dispel Scroll*
magic_items.description:
The Wizard reads aloud a charm of unbinding from an enchanted scroll.
Single use. A Wizard may use this scroll when attempting a Wizardly dispel. If they do so, roll an extra D6 when making the Dispel roll and discard the lowest result. However, if a double 1 is rolled on any two of the dice rolled, the Wizard is outclassed in the art.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Power Scroll*
magic_items.description:
Unfurling a scroll bearing runes of power can greatly increase the spell's power.
Single use. A Wizard may use this scroll when attempting to cast a spell. If they do so, roll an extra D6 when making the Casting roll and discard the lowest result. However, if a double 1 is rolled on any two of the dice rolled, the spell is miscast.*Extremely Common

magic_items.name: Lore Familiar
magic_items.description:
By creating a magical homunculus, a Wizard is able to better recall forgotten details of their studies.
The owner of a Lore Familiar does not randomly generate their spells. Instead, they may choose which spells they know from their chosen lore (including that lore's signature spell).

magic_items.name: Scroll of Disruption
magic_items.description:
When spoken aloud, the words upon this ancient scroll cause the Winds of Magic to bluster and the spells of the enemy to unravel.
Once per turn, the bearer of the Scroll of Disruption may re-roll a Dispel roll.*Note all dice are re-rolled, including any bonus or discard dice granted by special rules or magic items.

magic_items.name: Tome of Spellcraft
magic_items.description:
Reading aloud from a dusty tome of great age, the Wizard enunciates their spells with unrivalled clarity and precision.
Once per turn, the bearer of the Tome of Spellcraft may re-roll a Casting roll.*Note all dice are re-rolled, including any bonus or discard dice granted by special rules or magic items.

magic_items.name: Staff of Quietude
magic_items.description: With a gesture, the bearer of the Staff of Quietude calms the Winds of Magic, causing spells and conjurations to dissipate into the other.

magic_items.name: Wand of Jet
magic_items.description: Carved from brittle jet, this wand refines a Wizard's power, but is easily broken.
magic_items.restrictions: Wand of Jet(Arcane Item)45 pointsCarved from brittle jet, this wand refines a Wizard's power, but is easily broken.The bearer of the Wand of Jet may apply a +1 modifier to any of their Casting or Disp

magic_items.name: Scroll of Transmogrification*
magic_items.description: For a Wizard, there are few things better than the rush of joy felt upon turning a rival into a frog!
magic_items.restrictions: Scroll of Transmogrification*(Arcane Item)50 pointsFor a Wizard, there are few things better than the rush of joy felt upon turning a rival into a frog!Single use. The bearer may use this scroll inste

magic_items.name: Feedback Scroll
magic_items.description:
Arcane items are items that enhance a Wizard's magical powers in some fashion. Only Wizards can purchase Arcane Items.
When read aloud, this charm causes ætheric energy to flood back towards the enemy Wizard.
magic_items.restrictions: Arcane items are items that enhance a Wizard's magical powers in some fashion. Only Wizards can purchase Arcane Items.

magic_items.name: Monster Hunter's Tapestry
magic_items.description:
Those who have earned the right to fight under this standard have battled some of the Old World's most dangerous beasts and lived to tell the tale.
Enemy units may not make Stomp Attacks against a unit carrying the Monster Hunter's Tapestry.
magic_items.restrictions: Monster Hunter's Tapestry(Magic Standard)25 pointsThose who have earned the right to fight under this standard have battled some of the Old World's most dangerous beasts and lived to tell the tale.Ene

magic_items.name: The Blazing Banner
magic_items.description:
An eternal flame flickers about the weapons of those that march beneath the Blazing Banner.
A unit carrying the Blazing Banner gains the Flaming Attacks special rule.
magic_items.restrictions: The Blazing Banner(Magic Standard)25 pointsAn eternal flame flickers about the weapons of those that march beneath the Blazing Banner.A unit carrying the Blazing Banner gains the Flaming Attacks speci

magic_items.name: War Banner
magic_items.description:
A proud banner of great age. Those that march beneath the War Banner fight with grim resolve.
When calculating its combat result, a unit carrying the War Banner may claim an additional bonus of +1 combat result point.
magic_items.restrictions: War Banner(Magic Standard)25 pointsA proud banner of great age. Those that march beneath the War Banner fight with grim resolve.When calculating its combat result, a unit carrying the War Banner may c

magic_items.name: Banner of Swirling Wind
magic_items.description:
Gale-force winds surge around this standard so fiercely that arrows and shot are blown forcefully away from their target.
Any enemy model that shoots at a unit carrying the Banner of Swirling Wind suffers an additional -1 To Hit modifier.
magic_items.restrictions: Banner of Swirling Wind(Magic Standard)30 pointsGale-force winds surge around this standard so fiercely that arrows and shot are blown forcefully away from their target.Any enemy model that shoots at

magic_items.name: Rampaging Banner
magic_items.description:
As a matter of honour, those that fight beneath the Rampaging Banner are always first into the fray.
When a unit carrying the Rampaging Banner declares a charge, it may re-roll its Charge roll.
magic_items.restrictions: Rampaging Banner(Magic Standard)30 pointsAs a matter of honour, those that fight beneath the Rampaging Banner are always first into the fray.When a unit carrying the Rampaging Banner declares a charge

magic_items.name: Razor Standard
magic_items.description:
Hung with hundreds of wickedly sharp blades, an air of sharpness radiates outwards from the Razor Standard.
A unit carrying the Razor Standard gains the Armour Bane (2) special rule.
magic_items.restrictions: Razor Standard(Magic Standard)40 pointsHung with hundreds of wickedly sharp blades, an air of sharpness radiates outwards from the Razor Standard.A unit carrying the Razor Standard gains the Armour Ba

magic_items.name: Banner of Iron Resolve
magic_items.description:
Some units have the option to purchase magic standards. To do so, the unit must include a standard bearer. The only character that can carry a magic standard is your army's Battle Standard Bearer.
No regiment that stands beneath the Banner of Iron Resolve has ever fled from the foe.
A unit carrying the Banner of Iron Resolve gains the Stubborn special rule.
magic_items.restrictions: Some units have the option to purchase magic standards. To do so, the unit must include a standard bearer. The only character that can carry a magic standard is your army's Battle Standard Bearer.

magic_items.name: Banner of the Steadfast
magic_items.description: Woven into the bright fabric of this banner is a scene of unimaginable gallantry, a scene that serves to inspire those who fight in its shadow to hold their ground no matter the odds.
magic_items.restrictions: Banner of the Steadfast(Magic Standard)50 pointsWoven into the bright fabric of this banner is a scene of unimaginable gallantry, a scene that serves to inspire those who fight in its shadow to hold t

magic_items.name: Totem of Wrath
magic_items.description:
Cruel runes etched into the surface of this icon tremble with power, causing those around it to succumb to an animalistic ferocity.
During a turn in which it charged, models within a unit carrying the Totem of Wrath improve the Armour Piercing characteristic of their weapons by 1, and may re-roll any rolls To Wound of a natural 1.
magic_items.restrictions: Totem of Wrath(Magic Standard)50 pointsCruel runes etched into the surface of this icon tremble with power, causing those around it to succumb to an animalistic ferocity.During a turn in which it char

magic_items.name: Cacophonous Dirge
magic_items.description: Whilst their unit is within 18" of the bearer of the Cacophonous Dirge, enemy musicians cannot use their 'Onwards to Victory!', Steadying Rhythm or Quick Time rules.

magic_items.name: Goretooth*
magic_items.description: In addition to the Lores of Magic the bearer may normally know spells from, the bearer of a Goretooth may know spells from the Lore of Primal Magic.

magic_items.name: Jagged Dagger
magic_items.restrictions: Wizard that has joined a unit only
magic_items.description: Before making a Casting roll, the bearer of the Jagged Dagger may make a sacrifice. One model belonging to the bearer's unit is immediately removed from play as a casualty. The bearer may then modify their Casting roll by adding the Wounds characteristic of the sacrificed model to the result. Note that this is a modifier to the result of a roll — it does not negate a roll of a natural double 1.

magic_items.name: Skin of Man
magic_items.restrictions: Beastmen Chieftains and Beastmen Shamans whose troop type is 'infantry' only
magic_items.description: The wearer of the Skin of Man gains the Scouts and Vanguard special rules.

magic_items.name: The Soiled Tapestry
magic_items.description: A unit carrying the Soiled Tapestry gains the Hatred (all enemies) special rule. However, all enemy models Hate the unit carrying this banner.

magic_items.name: The Fur Of Sharggu
magic_items.description: May be worn with other armour. The wearer of the Fur of Sharggu improves their armour value by 2 (to a maximum of 2+) against non-magical shooting attacks.

magic_items.name: Banner of Outrage
magic_items.description: A unit carrying the Banner of Outrage may re-roll its Leadership test when testing for Primal Fury.

magic_items.name: Dark Heart*
magic_items.description: Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, the bearer of a Dark Heart can consume it. The model immediately recovers one lost Wound.

magic_items.name: Scimitar Of Skultar
magic_items.description: Range: Combat. S: S. AP: -1. Special Rules: Armour Bane (1), Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (2).

magic_items.name: Scourge Of The Burdened
magic_items.description: The wielder of the Scourge of the Burdened can cast the Call of the Wild spell from the Lore of Primal Magic as a Bound Spell, with a Power Level of 2.

magic_items.name: The Blackened Plate
magic_items.description: A suit of full plate armour. In addition, its wearer has a 3+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by an attack that has the Flaming Attacks special rule.

magic_items.name: Cornucopia Of Corpulence
magic_items.description: When the bearer's combat is chosen during Step 1.1 of any Choose & Fight Combat sub-phase, enemy models in base contact with the bearer of the Cornucopia of Corpulence must make a Toughness test. If this test is failed, they lose a single Wound.

magic_items.name: Daemon Heart
magic_items.description: When attempting a Wizardly dispel, the bearer of the Daemon Heart counts their Level of Wizardry as being one higher than that of the Wizard whose spell they are dispelling or, in the case of Bound Spells, one higher than that spell's Power Level. Note that this applies even if it would mean the bearer counts their Level of Wizardry as being lower than it actually is.

magic_items.name: Hagtree Fetish
magic_items.description: Whenever the bearer of the Hagtree Fetish successfully casts a Magic Missile, they may re-roll any failed rolls To Wound.

magic_items.name: Heavy Chaos Armour*
magic_items.description: A suit of heavy armour. May be purchased and worn by a Wizard, even if they do not have the option to be equipped with armour, a shield or barding, without penalty. The wearer of Chaos Armour has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.

magic_items.name: Horn of the First Beast
magic_items.description: Unless the bearer of the Horn of the First Beast is fleeing, any friendly unit that is within 18" of this model may use its Leadership characteristic when testing for Primal Fury.

magic_items.name: Rune of the True Beast
magic_items.description: Enemy models whose troop type is 'monster' suffer a -1 modifier to their Weapon Skill characteristic whilst within 6" of the bearer of the Rune of the True Beast.

magic_items.name: The Brass Cleaver
magic_items.description: Range: Combat. S: S. AP: -2. Special Rules: Magical Attacks. Enemy models are not permitted an armour, Ward or Regeneration save against an attack made with this weapon if the wielder rolls a natural 6 when making a roll To Wound.

magic_items.name: Vitriolic Totem
magic_items.description: A unit carrying the Vitriolic Totem gains the Poisoned Attacks special rule.

magic_items.name: Blood Of The Shadowgave*
magic_items.description: Single use. A Wizard may use this item instead of making a Wizardly dispel attempt. If they do so, the spell is automatically dispelled with no Dispel roll required. Note that a perfect invocation cannot be dispelled.

magic_items.name: Horn Of The Great Hunt
magic_items.description: Once per turn, after rolling to determine if a unit with the Ambushers special rule that is currently held in reserve as reinforcements arrives, and if the bearer of the Horn of the Great Hunt is on the battlefield and is not fleeing, you may apply a +1 or -1 modifier to the result.

magic_items.name: Hunter's Spear
magic_items.description: Range: 24". S: 6. AP: -3. Special Rules: Cumbersome, Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (2), Through & Through. Shoots like a bolt thrower, using the 'Through & Through' special rule.

magic_items.name: Full Plate Chaos Armour*
magic_items.description: A suit of full plate armour. May be purchased and worn by a Wizard, even if they do not have the option to be equipped with armour, a shield or barding, without penalty. The wearer of Chaos Armour has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.

magic_items.name: Manbane Standard
magic_items.description: All enemy units within 6" of the model carrying the Manbane Standard suffer a -1 modifier to their Leadership characteristic (to a minimum of 2).

magic_items.name: Mangelder
magic_items.description: Range: Combat. S: S+1. AP: -1. Special Rules: Magical Attacks, Terror. The wielder of the Mangelder causes Terror. Any enemy unit that suffers one or more unsaved wounds from the Mangelder suffers a -1 modifier to its Leadership characteristic (to a minimum of 2) until the end of the turn.

magic_items.name: Pelt of the Dark Young
magic_items.description: May be worn with other armour. The wearer of the Pelt of the Dark Young improves their armour value by 1 (to a maximum of 2+). In addition, its wearer (and any unit they have joined) has Magic Resistance (-2).

magic_items.name: The Axes Of Khorgor
magic_items.description: Range: Combat. S: S+1. AP: -2. Special Rules: Extra Attacks (+1), Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands. The wielder of the Axes of Khorgor may re-roll any rolls To Wound of a natural 1 made during the Combat phase.

magic_items.name: The Beast Banner
magic_items.description: A unit carrying the Beast Banner increases its maximum possible charge range by 3" and, when it makes a Charge roll, may apply a +D3 modifier to the result.

magic_items.name: The Plague Chalice
magic_items.description: If the bearer of the Plague Chalice rolls any natural double when making a Casting roll (not including rolls of a natural double 1), the spell is cast as a perfect invocation, regardless of its casting value or of any other modifiers. However, each time the bearer casts a perfect invocation, they suffer a Strength 4 hit with no armour save permitted (Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).

magic_items.name: Banner Of The Warped Moon
magic_items.description: During the Command sub-phase of their turn, if not engaged in combat, the bearer of this banner may attempt to unleash its power by making a Leadership test (using their own unmodified Leadership). If this test is passed, until your next Start of Turn sub-phase enemy units cannot use the Fly (X) special rule.

magic_items.name: Stone of Spite
magic_items.description: Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, if they are not engaged in combat, this character may use the Stone of Spite. Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, all Wizards (friend or foe) suffer a -2 modifier to their Casting rolls.

magic_items.name: Axe of Men
magic_items.description: Range: Combat. S: S. AP: -1. Special Rules: Killing Blow, Magical Attacks. Should the wielder of the Axe of Men kill an enemy character (including champions) in a challenge, they gain the Terror special rule for the remainder of the game.

magic_items.name: Chalice Of Dark Rain
magic_items.description: Single use. During the Command sub-phase of their turn, if not engaged in combat, the bearer of the Chalice of Dark Rain may drink from it. Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the range of all enemy missile weapons is halved (rounding fractions up) and enemy war machines cannot shoot.

magic_items.name: The Gore Banner
magic_items.description: If a unit wishes to declare a charge against a unit carrying the Gore Banner, it must first make a Leadership test. If this test is failed, the unit cannot charge. It does not move and is considered to have made a failed charge. If this test is passed, the unit can charge as normal.

magic_items.name: Totem of Rust
magic_items.description: Every model (friend or foe) within 6" of the model carrying the Totem of Rust suffers a -1 modifier to their Armour Save rolls.

magic_items.name: Primeval Club
magic_items.description: Range: Combat. S: * (equal to current Leadership of wielder). AP: -3. Special Rules: Hatred (Empire), Magical Attacks, Poisoned Attacks.

magic_items.name: The Black Maul
magic_items.description: Range: Combat. S: S+2. AP: -3. Special Rules: Extra Attacks (+D6), Magical Attacks. Each time a natural 6 is rolled for the Extra Attacks (+D6) special rule, the wielder of the Black Maul suffers a -1 modifier to their Weapon Skill characteristic (to a minimum of 1, and for the remainder of the game).

lore_of_beasts.name: Viletide
lore_of_beasts.effect: The target enemy unit suffers 5D6 Strength 1 hits, with no armour save permitted (Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).

lore_of_beasts.name: Devolve
lore_of_beasts.effect: The target enemy unit must immediately make a Leadership test. If this test is failed, it loses a number of Wounds equal to the amount by which it failed the test.

lore_of_beasts.name: Mantle of Ghorok
lore_of_beasts.effect: Until the end of this turn, the caster gains a +D6 modifier to their Strength and Attacks characteristics (to a maximum of 10). However, if a 6 is rolled, this modifier is lost and the caster instead loses a single Wound.

lore_of_primal_magic.name: Primordial Gloom
lore_of_primal_magic.effect: Remains in Play. Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is within 12" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template does not move and is treated as dangerous terrain. Whilst within 6" of the template, enemy units suffer a -1 modifier to their Leadership characteristic (to a minimum of 2) and treat open ground as difficult terrain.

lore_of_primal_magic.name: 1. Call Of The Wild
lore_of_primal_magic.effect: This spell can only target friendly models whose troop type is 'infantry' or 'cavalry'. Until the end of this turn, the target friendly unit gains the Reserve Move special rule.

lore_of_primal_magic.name: 2. In The Gloaming Wildwood
lore_of_primal_magic.effect: Remains in play. Whilst this spell is in play, the target enemy unit Fears all models in the caster's army (not including allies or mercenaries). Models in the caster's army that already have the Fear special rule cause Terror in the target unit instead. If this spell is cast, the effects of any other Hex previously cast on the target unit immediately expire.

lore_of_primal_magic.name: 3. Flock Of Doom
lore_of_primal_magic.effect: Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is directly over the centre of a unit the caster is engaged in combat with. Once placed, the template will scatter D3+1". Any enemy model whose base lies underneath the template's final position risks being hit and suffering a single Strength 3 hit with an AP of -. These hits have the Multiple Wounds (2) special rule.

lore_of_primal_magic.name: 4. Fury Of The Beast
lore_of_primal_magic.effect: Until the end of this turn, the caster and any unit they have joined gains the Fight in Extra Rank special rule.

lore_of_primal_magic.name: 5. Strangleroot
lore_of_primal_magic.effect: Draw a straight line, 4D6" in length, from the caster's base edge. Any enemy model whose base falls under this line suffers D3 Strength 3 hits, each with an AP of -2.

lore_of_primal_magic.name: 6. 'Neath The Shaden Wodespan
lore_of_primal_magic.effect: Remains in play. Place a large (5") blast template so that its central hole is within 15" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template does not move and is treated as dangerous terrain over which no line of sight can be drawn. Whilst within 6" of the template, enemy units suffer a -2 modifier to their Ballistic Skill characteristic (to a minimum of 1). Any enemy unit that ends its movement within 3" of the template must immediately make a Panic test. If this test is failed, the unit will flee directly away from the nearest enemy unit which is not itself fleeing.
